articles.title: Pound Sterling softens as markets await Fed and BoE rate decisions
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair trades in negative territory around 1.3525 during the early Asian session on Tuesday. The Pound Sterling (GBP) softens against the US Dollar (USD) as traders prefer to wait on the sidelines ahead of the Federal Reserve (Fed) and the Bank of England (BoE) later this week.

articles.title: Bank of Japan seen on hold as focus shifts to timing of next hike
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan (BoJ) will announce its monetary policy decision on Tuesday, at around 3:00 GMT. The BoJ is widely expected to deliver a hawkish hold, keeping the benchmark interest rate unchanged at 0.75% while also hinting at a willingness to hike rates.

articles.title: Australian Dollar gains on RBA rate hike likelihood
articles.expand: AUD/USD extends its gains for the third consecutive day, trading around 0.7190 during the Asian hours on Monday. The pair holds gains as the Australian Dollar (AUD) receives support, rising expectations of the Reserve Bank of Australia’s (RBA) rate hikes.

articles.title: PBOC is expected to set the USD/CNY reference rate at 6.8282 – Reuters estimate
articles.expand: The People’s Bank of China is due to set the daily USD/CNY reference rate at around 0115 GMT (2115 US Eastern time), a fixing that remains one of the most closely watched signals in Asian foreign exchange markets. China operates a managed floating exchange rate system, under which the renminbi (yuan) is allowed to trade within a prescribed band aro...

articles.title: Easter discounts cool UK shop prices but Iran war inflation threat looms large
articles.expand: UK shop price inflation eased to 1.0% in April from 1.2% in March as Easter discounts helped retailers stimulate spending, though the BRC warned Middle East cost pressures are fast approaching. articles.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessent: Working with Iranian airlines risks sanctions
articles.expand: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned that the United States (US) will place sanctions on anyone conducting business with sanctioned Iranian airlines as commercial flights resume from Tehran, the Wall Street Journal reported on Monday.

articles.title: Gold tumbles below $4,700, Iran’s Hormuz Strait proposal and Fed rate decision in focus
articles.expand: Gold price (XAU/USD) slumps to near $4,685 during the early Asian session on Tuesday. Markets turn to "wait-and-see" mode ahead of the US Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate decision and shifting developments in the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: NZD/USD climbs above 0.59 as hot CPI fuels RBNZ hike bets ahead of Fed
articles.expand: NZD/USD posted limited gains of around 0.4% on Monday, settling close to 0.5905 but stalling just below the 0.5925 area that capped last week's recovery.

articles.title: BOJ expected to hold rates steady as Iran conflict complicates tightening path
articles.expand: The BOJ is expected to hold rates at 0.75% as the Iran war clouds its tightening path, with Governor Ueda's press conference the key watch for signals on the next hike. articles.title: AUD/USD rallies toward 0.7200 as Aussie gains on easing war fears
articles.expand: The Australian Dollar soars some 0.53% on Monday, with the Greenback staying firm amid uncertainty regarding the outcome of the US-Iran conflict as Washington reviews Tehran’s last proposal to end the war. At the time of writing, the AUD/USD trades at 0.7185 after bouncing off daily lows of 0.7125.

articles.title: Markets Weekly Outlook - Can earnings outweigh geopolitical headwinds & Central Bank decisions?
articles.expand: Global markets face a pivotal week defined by surging oil prices amid US-Iran tensions, record-high US equities driven by tech and earnings optimism, and critical central bank decisions. The Fed, ECB, and BoE grapple with the "balancing problem" of inflation vs. growth, while the BoJ remains the 'wild card' for a surprise rate hike.
